steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between the adjective "renowned" and the proper name "Fu Pei-mei." The phrase in the blank is an appositive-style descriptor identifying who Fu Pei-mei is. Standard English does not place a comma between a descriptive title and the name it immediately precedes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'chef and culinary instructor,': The trailing comma incorrectly separates the descriptor from the name "Fu Pei-mei." No comma belongs between a title phrase and the name it identifies. ✗
  • Choice B — 'chef and culinary instructor': No internal comma, no trailing comma. "Renowned chef and culinary instructor Fu Pei-mei" follows the standard pattern for a title preceding a name (compare: "renowned chef Julia Child"). ✓
  • Choice C — 'chef, and culinary instructor': The comma after "chef" is incorrect. "Chef and culinary instructor" is a two-element compound with no comma needed before "and." ✗
  • Choice D — 'chef, and culinary instructor—': Both the internal comma and the em dash are incorrect. The dash would create a jarring interruption right before the subject of the sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
